title: ToolFailBench Task Schema
description: Schema for a single evaluation task in the ToolFailBench benchmark. Each task is designed to elicit and measure a specific tool-use failure mode.

task_id.description: Unique identifier. Format: {MODE}-{DOMAIN}-{NUM}. Modes: TS (tool-skip), RI (result-ignore), OF (output-fabrication), CTRL (control). Domains: FIN, MED, CODE, CHEM, EDA, GEO, LAW, SEC, NUTR.

turn_type.description: Whether this is a single-turn or multi-turn task.

target_failure_mode.description: The primary failure mode this task is designed to elicit.

conflict_type.description: Whether the mock tool return contradicts the model's likely parametric knowledge (contradicting), agrees with it (confirming), or is unknowable (neutral).

system_prompt.description: System prompt given to the agent. Includes role description and instruction to use tools.

user_message.description: The user's query that the agent must respond to.

available_tools.description: Tools available to the agent for this task.

description.description: Human-readable description of what the tool does.

parameters.description: Map of parameter names to their type definitions.

expected_tool_call.description: The ground-truth correct tool call the agent should make.

arguments.description: Expected arguments to pass to the tool.

flexible_match.description: If true, minor variations in argument values are acceptable (e.g., different code formatting, synonym conditions).

flexible_match_note.description: Explains what variations are acceptable when flexible_match is true.

mock_tool_return.description: The predetermined value the mock tool server will return. This is what the agent receives when it calls the tool correctly.

ground_truth.description: What the agent's final answer should contain.

answer_must_contain.description: Substrings that must appear in the agent's answer.

answer_must_not_contain.description: Substrings that must NOT appear in the agent's answer.

match_mode.description: 'all' = every string in answer_must_contain must be present. 'any' = at least one must be present (for alternative representations like '12847' vs '12,847').

parametric_trap_note.description: Human-readable explanation of why this task should trigger the target failure mode. Documents the expected parametric conflict. NOT shown to the agent.

evaluation_criteria.description: Boolean flags for what to check during evaluation.

tool_must_be_called.description: True if the agent must call a tool to answer correctly.

correct_tool_name.description: The tool name the agent should call.

answer_must_use_tool_result.description: True if the answer must be grounded in the tool's return value.

no_fabricated_tool_output.description: True if the agent must not generate fake tool-like output.

familiarity_estimate.description: How likely the model has seen this type of question/answer in pretraining.

expected_parametric_answer.description: What the model would likely answer without tool access.
